data.title: Fitts’s Law
data.description: Predicts how quickly a user can interact with target areas, influencing WhatsApp interface design.
data.technical_spec: Buttons for frequent actions like send, attach, and record are easily accessible for quicker interactions.

data.title: Hick's Law
data.description: Suggests reducing choice delays to minimize cognitive load, here applied by maintaining a clean interface.
data.technical_spec: WhatsApp prioritizes essential functions on the chat screen to prevent user overload.

data.title: Jakob’s Law
data.description: Design consistency with other platforms to meet user expectations, seen in WhatsApp's interface.
data.technical_spec: Familiar UI elements across popular messaging applications help reduce the learning curve.

data.title: Miller's Law
data.description: The UI is designed to handle segment information into chunks to enhance user comprehension.
data.technical_spec: Use of visual grouping in chat selection, notification settings, and media browsing.

data.title: Peak-End Rule
data.description: Designs experiences to ensure positive user impressions, affecting message delivery and read receipt features.
data.technical_spec: Optimized delivery ticks and read receipts provide a clear end to the conversation flow.
